articles.title: Sam Alito Complains That Dog Ate His Homework — See Generally
articles.content:
萨姆·阿利托抱怨说狗吃了他的作业——详见上文

萨姆·阿利托怒喷自己明明能提出更好的论点（剧透：他根本提不出来）：本周，最高法院大法官阿利托打破惯例，在法庭上发牢骚，称索托马约尔大法官的异议让他在穆林诉阿尔奥托罗案中显得很糟糕——该案的核心是抽空了纳粹大屠杀时期的庇护保护。

人人都喜欢刀刃向外的快感，直到刀刃转向自己：当明星合伙人拿到近4000万美元的薪酬包时，顾问们估计10%到30%的合伙人在年中评审时将面临降薪，因为取代等级制的薪酬体系是一个将回报集中在顶层的制度，而总得有人站在底层。

本·萨斯要么完全视而不见，要么是世上最有趣的人：这位前参议员辩称，在最高法院安装摄像头可能会让法院显得有政治色彩。

斯坦·伍德沃德给法官发送措辞强硬的便函；法官发回日程安排：斯坦·伍德沃德——司法部最令人尴尬的文件的御用签名人——发送了一份两页的"通知"，告知法官莱奥妮·布林克玛她最近的命令是"不必要的"。她并不觉得好笑。

金钱买不到的最微弱赞美：比尔·巴尔在《华尔街日报》发表专栏文章，论证托德·布兰奇应该被确认为司法部长，因为他在"特朗普总统领导下管理该部门的能力不逊于任何人。"

沼泽真实存在，就在林肯纪念堂：特朗普花费数百万纳税人的钱把倒影池涂成蓝色，现在让·皮罗的司法部正在因为总统的池子维护失职而逮捕公民。

特朗普最爱的律所喜欢涨薪：沙利文与克伦威尔——如今唐纳德·特朗普的首选律所——显然已经匹配了新的米尔班克薪资标准。

机器给出了正确答案；法院适用了法律：一份人工智能转录文本意外地将被告那句含混的"我需要在这里有律师吗？"转录成了对第六修正案律师代理权的明确援引，但法官认定没那么明确，于是蒸发了被告的米兰达权利。

那篇让我笑出声的法律评论文章（一次，然后又因为他解释了笑点而笑了第二次）：一篇佛蒙特法律评论的文章论证幽默能改善法律教学和知识保留，文章本身足够有趣以证明论点，然后通过在两个不错的笑话后面加上"看出我做了什么吗？"来摧毁自己的可信度——颇具诗意地，这是一个关于时机为何重要的教训。

本文首次发布于Above the Law。
articles.summary:
Sam Alito Huffed That He Totally Could’ve Made Better Arguments (Spoiler: He Could Not Have): Alito broke protocol at the Supreme Court this week to whine from the bench that Sotomayor’s dissent made him look bad for gutting Holocaust-era asylum protections in Mullin v. Al Otro Lado.

Everybody Loves Living By The Sword Until The Sword Turns Around: While star partners are hauling in packages approaching $40 million, consultants estimate that 10 to 30 percent of partners are heading into mid-year reviews with a pay cut coming, because lockstep’s replacement is a system that concentrates rewards at the top and somebody has to be at the bottom.

Ben Sasse Is Either Totally Oblivious Or The Funniest Man Alive: The former senator argues that adding cameras might make the Supreme Court seem political.

Stan Woodward Sends Judge Strongly Worded Note; The Judge Sends Back Scheduling Order: Stan Woodward, the DOJ’s go-to signature for its most embarassing filings, sent a two-page “notice” informing Judge Leonie Brinkema that her recent order was “unnecessary.” She was not amused.

The Faintest Praise Money Can’t Buy: Bill Barr wrote a Wall Street Journal op-ed arguing that Todd Blanche should be confirmed as AG because he “will run the department as effectively as anyone could under President Trump.”

The Swamp Is Real And It’s At The Lincoln Memorial: Trump spent millions of taxpayer dollars painting the Reflecting Pool blue, and now Jeanine Pirro’s Justice Department is arresting citizens for the president’s pool maintenance failures.

Trump’s Favorite Law Firm Loves A Pay Raise: Sullivan & Cromwell — Donald Trump’s preferred law firm these days — has apparently matched the new Milbank salary scale.

The Machine Had The Right Answer; The Court Had The Law: An AI transcript accidentally rendered a defendant’s ambiguous “Do I need to have an attorney here?” as a clear invocation of his Sixth Amendment right to counsel, but the judge decided it wasn’t THAT clear and evaporated the defendant’s Miranda rights.

The Law Review Article That Made Me Chuckle (Once, Then Twice When He Explained The Joke): A Vermont Law Review article argues that humor improves legal pedagogy and retention, is itself funny enough to prove the thesis, and then undermines its own credibility by following two of its better jokes with “See what I did there?” — which is, somewhat poetically, a lesson in why timing matters.

The post Sam Alito Complains That Dog Ate His Homework — See Generally appeared first on Above the Law.

articles.title: S&C Takes The Salary Plunge — See Also
articles.content:
**Sullivan & Cromwell提高律师薪资：** 可能达到Milbank标准。

**Brendan Carr想要打击"The View"：** 过去26年都没问题。

**这是讽刺吗？可惜不是：** Ben Sasse对最高法院摄像头的调侃是认真的。

**人质谈判：** Bill Barr希望参议院确认Todd Blanche。否则后果自负。

**1万美元AI错误：** 纽约上诉法院因伪造引述和判例而处罚。
articles.summary:
Sullivan & Crowell Raises Associate Salaries: Probably to the Milbank scale.

Brendan Carr Wants To Crack Down On “The View”: Wasn’t a problem over the last 26 years.

Is This Satire? If only: Ben Sasse’s creed against SCOTUS cameras is dead serious.

Hostage Negotiations: Bill Barr wants Todd Blanche confirmed. Or Else.

$10K AI Error: New York Appellate court issues sanctions after fake cites and holdings.

The post S&C Takes The Salary Plunge — See Also appeared first on Above the Law.

articles.title: Democracy Isn’t Always Easy
articles.content:
编辑注：欢迎来到我们的每日栏目“每日趣味问答”！

哪位开国元勋——一位曾担任首届最高法院大法官的律师——在1779年10月遭到暴民袭击，但他也是独立宣言序言发表后极少数曾在公开场合引用该文件的人之一（根据詹姆斯·麦迪逊的笔记，此人在费城宪法 convention 会议上宣读了该文件）？

提示：这位不太知名的历史人物“坚信人民自治的原则，以至于在武装暴徒破门而入、试图杀害他之后，他反而更加坚定地捍卫这一原则”，传记者杰西·韦格曼如是说。

答案见下页。

本文首次发布于 Above the Law。
articles.summary:
Ed. Note: Welcome to our daily feature Trivia Question of the Day!

Which founding father, an attorney who would go on to serve on the very first Supreme Court, was targeted by rioters in October 1779, but was also one of the only ones known to quote from the Declaration of Independence’s preamble in public after 1776 (according to James Madison’s notes, the person read from the document on the floor of the Constitutional Convention in Philadelphia)?

Hint: The lesser-known historical figure was “so committed to the principle of popular self-rule that he doubled down on it even after an armed mob broke down his front door and tried to kill him,” according to biographer Jesse Wegman.

See the answer on the next page.

The post Democracy Isn’t Always Easy appeared first on Above the Law.

articles.title: Sullivan & Cromwell Jumps On Biglaw Salary Raise Train
articles.content:
特朗普最青睐的律师事务所历来遵循纽约市场惯例确定薪资水平，但其奖金发放一直秘而不宣。当Milbank率先提高薪资标准后，大多数大型律师事务所都按兵不动、观望徘徊，但我们却获悉Sullivan & Cromwell已率先行动，跟进了这一新标准。

该律所单独公布薪酬调整方案，因此我们无法确认其是否完全按照新标准执行，但根据知情人士的初步透露，S&C似乎已全面跟进。

因此，尽管我们无法保证S&C对所有层级员工都完全适用这一标准，在此还是要提醒读者Milbank确立的新标准内容：加薪自7月1日起生效。

如果您有任何其他律所的薪资消息，请尽快将贵所的备忘录发送给我们（邮件主题：“[律所名称]薪资”），或致电我们（646-820-8477）。请附上备忘录原文。如不方便转发PDF或Word文件，也可拍照通过短信或邮件发送。

如需订阅ATL的薪资与奖金提醒，请在帖子下方输入您的邮箱地址。如果您之前已订阅奖金提醒，无需任何操作。每次奖金公告发布后数分钟内，您将收到邮件通知。

Joe Patrice是Above the Law的高级编辑及《像律师一样思考》播客的联合主持人。欢迎通过邮件发送任何线索、问题或意见。如您对法律、政治和大学体育新闻感兴趣，请在Twitter或Bluesky上关注他。

本文《Sullivan & Cromwell加入大型律所薪资上调潮》首次发布于Above the Law。
articles.summary:
Donald Trump’s favorite law firm keeps its bonuses firmly squirreled away in a black box, but historically follows the New York prevailing market when it comes to salaries. While most Biglaw firms have kept their powder dry in response to Milbank raising the stakes, we’re hearing that Sullivan & Cromell have taken the plunge and followed the new standard.

The firm announces its compensation changes individually, so we can’t confirm the whole scale matched, but based on early tipsters it seems the firm has followed the scale all the way up.

So, with the full caveat that we can’t say this is accurate for S&C for all classes, we’ll remind readers that this is the Milbank standard:

The raises are effective July 1.

Please help us help you when it comes to salary news at other firms. As soon as your firm’s memo comes out, please email it to us (subject line: “[Firm Name] Salary”) or text us (646-820-8477). Please include the memo if available. You can take a photo of the memo and send it via text or email if you don’t want to forward the original PDF or Word file.

And if you’d like to sign up for ATL’s Salary & Bonus Alerts, please scroll down and enter your email address in the box below this post. If you previously signed up for the bonus alerts, you don’t need to do anything. You’ll receive an email notification within minutes of each bonus announcement that we publish.

Joe Patrice is a senior editor at Above the Law and co-host of Thinking Like A Lawyer. Feel free to email any tips, questions, or comments. Follow him on Twitter or Bluesky if you’re interested in law, politics, and a healthy dose of college sports news.

The post Sullivan & Cromwell Jumps On Biglaw Salary Raise Train appeared first on Above the Law.

articles.title: Appeals Court Orders $10K In Sanctions Over AI-Related Filing Mistakes
articles.content:
你不必走很远就能找到关于大语言模型在法律工作中宝贵用例的故事。例如，智能使用人工智能可以减少你花在琐碎工作上的时间，让你有更多精力专注于真正“法律”的工作部分。不幸的是，它无法阻止你成为一个笨蛋。在经历了一段宽松的采用期后，律师们发现自己正处于“玩火自焚”时代的后半段。善意提醒——在法官也在你口袋里烧出一个几千美元的洞之前，从他们的错误中吸取教训。NY Daily Record对此进行了报道：

纽约州上诉法院已下令对一名律师及其律师事务所处以总计10,500美元的货币制裁，此前该律师在一起民事诉讼中提交了一份简报，其中包含明显由人工智能工具生成的虚假引用。

州最高法院第二部门的 Appellate Division 下令律师 Michael Sanders 支付 8,000 美元，其律师事务所支付 2,500 美元给纽约州律师客户保护基金。

……LaSalle 写道，Sanders 提交了一份“在大语言模型人工智能协助下准备的简报，其中包含对不存在案件的引用、完全违背实际法律的虚构 Court of Appeals 语录，以及对某些真实案件实际裁决内容的虚假陈述”。

如果做好工作的道德义务还不够有力，我完全支持从律师口袋里掏钱。确保你引用的案件 1) 是真实的，以及 2) 确实支持你所声称的内容，这不是高标准，我们很高兴看到在追究律师未能为客户提供适当服务方面开始减少纵容。你的客户付钱给你做律师的工作是有原因的，而不是让你把问题扔给 Claude 来弄清楚下一步该怎么做。如果你在宣称自己是律师，但实际上只是穿着西装的 LLM 中间人，这确实会并且应该降低客户对你实际在做你声称在收费工作的信任度。

好消息是 Sanders 欣然承认了自己的错误。我们希望这是他最后一次陷入此类麻烦。更重要的是，我们希望他的故事能成为让每个人都需要检查自己引用的警钟。记住：如果权威看起来太好以至于不真实，那很可能确实不真实。

律师和律师事务所因 AI 错误在法庭文件中受制裁 [NY Daily Record]

Chris Williams 于 2021 年 6 月成为 Above the Law 的社交媒体经理和副编辑。在加入团队之前，他在 Facebook 群组“法学院表情包 for Edgy T14s”中兼职担任小有名气的 Memelord™。他在密苏里州熬了足够长的时间，毕业于圣路易斯华盛顿大学法学院。他曾是一名造船工人，正在学习游泳，对修辞学、斯宾诺莎主义者和幽默感兴趣。重新开始骑自行车也无妨。你可以通过电子邮件 christopherrashadwilliams@gmail.com 或 Tweet/Bluesky @WritesForRent 联系他。

这篇《上诉法院因 AI 相关文件错误下令 10,000 美元制裁》的帖子首次出现在 Above the Law 上。
articles.summary:
You don’t have to go far to find a story on the valuable use cases of LLMs in legal work. For example, smart use of AI can reduce your time spent doing grunt work, freeing you up to focus more on the actually “legal” part of your job. Unfortunately, it cannot stop you from being a dunce. After a lax period of adoption, attorneys are finding themselves squarely in the latter part of the fuck around and find out era. Word to the wise — learn from their mistakes before a judge burns a couple thousand dollar hole in your pocket too. NY Daily Record has coverage:

A New York state appeals court has ordered monetary sanctions totaling $10,500 for an attorney and his law firm after the attorney submitted a brief in a civil lawsuit that contained fake citations apparently generated by an artificial intelligence tool.

The Appellate Division of state Supreme Court, Second Department, ordered attorney Michael Sanders to pay $8,000 and his firm to pay $2,500 to the Lawyers’ Fund for Client Protection of the State of New York.
…
Sanders submitted a brief “prepared with the assistance of generative artificial intelligence, containing citations to nonexistent cases, fictitious purported Court of Appeals quotations wholly contrary to actual law, and misrepresentations about what certain real cases actually held or decided,” LaSalle wrote.

If ethical obligations to do your job well aren’t strong enough, I’m all for hitting lawyers in their pockets. Ensuring that the cases you cite 1) are real, and 2) actually hold the things you say they do is not a high bar and I’m glad we’re starting to see less coddling when it comes to holding attorneys accountable for underserving their clients. There’s a reason your clients are paying you to do the work of lawyering rather than just throwing their problems into Claude to figure out where to go next. If you’re proclaiming to be a lawyer when you’re actually just an LLM middleman in a suit, it will and should lower client trust that you’re actually doing what you claim to be billing for.

The good news is that Sanders readily owned up to his mistakes. We hope this is the last time he finds himself in trouble like this. Stronger still, we hope that his story will be the kick in the shin everyone else needs to go over their cites. Remember: if the authority looks a little too good to be true, it very well may be.

Attorney And Law Firm Sanctioned For AI Mistakes In Court Filing [NY Daily Record]

Chris Williams became a social media manager and assistant editor for Above the Law in June 2021. Prior to joining the staff, he moonlighted as a minor Memelord™ in the Facebook group Law School Memes for Edgy T14s .  He endured Missouri long enough to graduate from Washington University in St. Louis School of Law. He is a former boat builder who is learning to swim and is interested in rhetoric, Spinozists and humor. Getting back in to cycling wouldn’t hurt either. You can reach him by email at christopherrashadwilliams@gmail.com and by Tweet/Bluesky at @WritesForRent.

The post Appeals Court Orders $10K In Sanctions Over AI-Related Filing Mistakes appeared first on Above the Law.
